text:
Once upon a time in sleepy Oak Grove, a curious squirrel named Sammy loved collecting acorns. One crisp autumn morning, Sammy had gathered enough acorns to fill his basket. As he scampered up his favorite oak tree, he imagined grand castles built just from them.

On his way downstairs, Sammy dashed across the dew-covered lawn. He stopped by the birch shed for a cozy nap. Midnight approached, and still no owl. That night, sleeping tight in his nest, Sammy fantasized about flying circles around cities filled with lights.

The next day, a gentle rain started falling. Sammy climbed a sturdy elm branch. On purpose, he glided down to the drippy maple leaf garden before scooping up several glossy acorns. The journey home was slow, but he felt proud.

By twilight, it was dark enough for dreams. Sammy drifted off to sleep feeling satisfied with his stash. And dreams became reality—for yesterday evening’s moonlit walk uncovered hidden compartments of an antique cabinet.
